2008-07-01.title: Hackers Tag Lithuanian Web Sites With Soviet Symbols
2008-07-01.abstract: The Web sites were vandalized two weeks after Lithuania, a former Soviet republic, outlawed the display of Soviet symbols, a ban that touched off new tensions with Russia.

2008-07-01.title: U.N. Personnel Increasingly Under Attack, Study Finds
2008-07-01.abstract: The organization is perceived by some as a tool of powerful members, rather than an unbiased advocate for all nations, said a veteran diplomat.

2008-07-01.title: Wounded Iraqi Forces Say They’ve Been Abandoned
2008-07-01.abstract: Iraqi veterans described their government’s treatment of them as at best indifferent and at worst vindictive.

2008-07-01.title: Undeterred by Criticism, Mugabe Joins Peers at African Union Meeting
2008-07-01.abstract: Robert Mugabe asserted his place among African leaders after a violence-stained runoff vote in Zimbabwe.

2008-07-01.title: Ragtag Insurgency Gains a Lifeline From Al Qaeda
2008-07-01.abstract: A group of Algerian militants has been transformed from a nationalist insurgency to a potent ally of Al Qaeda.

2008-07-02.title: Iraq’s Wounded Veterans
2008-07-02.abstract: Iraqi soldiers and police officers have been wounded in greater numbers than U.S. veterans, health workers say, and have been treated far worse by their government.

2008-07-02.title: French Army Chief Resigns Over Bullet Debacle
2008-07-02.abstract: The chief of staff of the French Army resigned, two days after a soldier fired bullets instead of blanks during a military exhibition, injuring 17 people.

2008-07-02.title: Russia: New Charges Against Jailed Tycoon
2008-07-02.abstract: Prosecutors have announced new charges against Mikhail B. Khodorkovsky, former chairman of Yukos Oil, which was once Russia’s largest private company.

2008-07-02.title: Ruth Cardoso, Ex-First Lady of Brazil, Dies at 77
2008-07-02.abstract: Ruth Vilaça Corrêa Leite Cardoso was a Brazilian anthropologist who carved out a career as one of her country’s most respected intellectuals and feminists.

2008-07-02.title: China Resumes Negotiations With Envoys of Dalai Lama
2008-07-02.abstract: Chinese officials and senior envoys of the Dalai Lama opened their latest round of negotiations over Tibet.

2008-07-03.title: Opposition Rejects Call to Start Talks With Mugabe
2008-07-03.abstract: Zimbabwe’s opposition leader, Morgan Tsvangirai, rejected the African Union’s call for talks with Robert Mugabe, citing continuing government-backed violence.

2008-07-03.title: Company Never Forgot Captive Workers
2008-07-03.abstract: For years, Northrop Grumman, a leading military contractor, kept a Web page about the three employees held hostage in Colombia, who were freed on Wednesday.

2008-07-03.title: Assisted Suicide of Healthy 79-Year-Old Renews German Debate on Right to Die
2008-07-03.abstract: A woman’s desire to avoid life in a nursing home forces a country to confront the thorny ethical issue and casts an assisted-suicide advocate as Germany’s Jack Kevorkian.

2008-07-03.title: Recognition for a People Who Faded as Japan Grew
2008-07-03.abstract: Only a few weeks ago the Japanese government finally, and unexpectedly, recognized the Ainu, who live on Japan’s northernmost island, as an “indigenous people.”

2008-07-03.title: Mongolia Enforces Martial Law in Capital Amid Political Unrest
2008-07-03.abstract: Armed soldiers enforced martial law on the streets of Mongolia’s capital on Wednesday, a day after five people were killed as hundreds angered by election results rioted.

2008-07-04.title: A Surprised France Prepares a Welcome
2008-07-04.abstract: A jubilant France prepares to welcome Ingrid Betancourt, the French-Colombian politician freed on Wednesday after six years of captivity by rebels in the Colombian jungle.

2008-07-04.title: Bush to Attend Opening Ceremony in Beijing
2008-07-04.abstract: The president’s decision was fraught with international symbolism that quickly drew criticism from advocates for human rights.

2008-07-04.title: An American Nest Returns to Its Historic Perch in Germany
2008-07-04.abstract: The opening of the U.S. Embassy in Berlin marks the final chapter in more than a decade of an often bitter process between the city and American diplomats.

2008-07-04.title: China Agrees to More Talks on Tibet
2008-07-04.abstract: Tibetan envoys left Beijing on Thursday after two days of private meetings with a pledge for more talks.

2008-07-04.title: Bold Colombia Rescue Built on Rebel Group’s Disarray
2008-07-04.abstract: The Colombian military duped FARC guerrillas in a raid that freed 15 hostages, an action that some analysts suggested might help push the rebels to negotiate for peace.

2008-07-05.title: Iran Responds Obliquely to Nuclear Plan
2008-07-05.abstract: Iran failed to address the central issue of whether it would halt its uranium enrichment activities, officials said.

2008-07-05.title: Frontier Years Give Might to Ex-Guerrilla’s Words
2008-07-05.abstract: Ahmed Rashid at home in Lahore, Pakistan. An expert on the Taliban, Mr. Rashid has turned out to be something of a prophet in the region, but mostly of the Cassandra type.

2008-07-05.title: Betancourt, in France, Details Her Captivity
2008-07-05.abstract: Two days after her rescue from the clutches of guerrillas in the Colombian jungle, Ingrid Betancourt shared some painful details of her long ordeal.

2008-07-05.title: Flights Begin From China to Taiwan
2008-07-05.abstract: The flights, the latest breakthrough in warming cross-strait relations, mark the first time that “ordinary” Chinese citizens will be allowed to visit Taiwan as tourists.

2008-07-05.title: Colombia Releases Video of Jungle Rescue
2008-07-05.abstract: Officials denied paying a ransom to the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia for the release of hostages.

2008-07-06.title: Police Kill 9 Inmates During Prison Riot in Syria
2008-07-06.abstract: Syrian military police officers fired on Islamist inmates during a riot at a prison outside Damascus.

2008-07-06.title: Ancient Tablet Ignites Debate on Messiah and Resurrection
2008-07-06.abstract: The writing on an ancient stone may contribute to a re-evaluation of popular and scholarly views of Jesus.

2008-07-06.title: Thai Museum at Angkor Draws Tourists, and Criticism
2008-07-06.abstract: With few artifacts remaining at the site of the Angkor temples, a Thai company’s new museum there is being criticized for everything from politics to its displays.

2008-07-06.title: Corrections: For the Record
2008-07-06.abstract: An article last Sunday about new American economic sanctions to be imposed on Zimbabwe following that country’s presidential runoff election on June 27, which was widely criticized as fraudulent and coercive, misspelled the surname of the director of the business and human rights program of Human Rights Watch, who said the sanctions could have a significant impact on Zimbabwe’s mining industry. He is Arvind Ganesan, not Ganesam.

2008-07-06.title: A Side Competition in Beijing: Island vs. Mainland
2008-07-06.abstract: As Taiwan’s athletes prepare for the Olympics, its officials fear that China will use the Games for political purposes.

2008-07-07.title: Bush, Preparing for Talks, Defends Olympics Decision
2008-07-07.abstract: Yasuo Fukuda, the prime minister of Japan, joined President Bush in saying he would attend the opening ceremony of the Olympic Games in Beijing.

2008-07-07.title: U.S. Helps Remove Uranium From Iraq
2008-07-07.abstract: The removal of hundreds of tons of natural uranium from the country’s main nuclear site represents one of the final chapter in the dismantling of Saddam Hussein’s nuclear program.

2008-07-07.title: Afghans Say New U.S. Strike Killed Civilians
2008-07-07.abstract: Local officials said that an American airstrike killed at least 27 civilians at a wedding, but coalition officials disputed the report.

2008-07-07.title: Cities Near Beijing Close Factories to Improve Air for Olympics
2008-07-07.abstract: The planned shutdowns of 40 factories near Beijing are one piece of a plan that is expected to result in many more temporary factory closings in the region during the Games.

2008-07-07.title: Decades Later, Toxic Sludge Torments Bhopal
2008-07-07.abstract: Hundreds of tons of waste still languish on the grounds of a pesticide factory in India, the site of a notorious disaster.

2008-07-08.title: Toxic Waste Plagues Bhopal
2008-07-08.abstract: Hundreds of tons of waste still languish on the old grounds of the Union Carbide pesticide factory in Bhopal, India, nearly a quarter-century after a poison gas leak killed thousands.

2008-07-08.title: In Medvedev, Bush Sees ‘a Smart Guy’
2008-07-08.abstract: President Bush’s comments came after he met for over an hour with Russia’s new president, Dimitri A. Medvedev.

2008-07-08.title: South Korean President Fires 3 Cabinet Ministers
2008-07-08.abstract: The cabinet reshuffle aimed at restoring public trust in his four-month-old government which has been shaken by weeks of protests against American beef imports.

2008-07-08.title: Canada: Freed Man to Receive Millions
2008-07-08.abstract: A man who was convicted of a murder and rape 49 years ago that he did not commit will receive about $6.4 million in compensation, the Ontario government said.

2008-07-08.title: Iraqi Favors Short Security Pact With U.S.
2008-07-08.abstract: With elections nearing in both countries, Iraq’s prime minister confirmed that his government was leaning toward a short-term agreement.

2008-07-09.title: U.S. and Czechs Sign Accord on Missile Shield
2008-07-09.abstract: The accord will allow the Pentagon to deploy part of its missile shield on territory once occupied by Soviet troops.

2008-07-09.title: India: Communists Desert Premier
2008-07-09.abstract: The Communist parties that have been part of Manmohan Singh’s parliamentary majority said they were withdrawing support because of the proposed nuclear deal with the U.S.

2008-07-09.title: Afghan Bombing Sends Stark Message to India
2008-07-09.abstract: In condemning the embassy attack, the Indian prime minister, Manmohan Singh, said that his country’s engagement in Afghanistan would continue.

2008-07-09.title: Two Concerns for Olympics: Air and Access
2008-07-09.abstract: Pollution and media access remain uncertainties as Beijing hustles to get ready for the opening ceremonies.

2008-07-09.title: A Holiday From Russia With Love
2008-07-09.abstract: The Day of Family, Love and Fidelity is a holiday that the Russian government set up in its latest attempt to halt population loss in the country.

2008-07-10.title: Peacekeepers in Sudan Lose 7 in Ambush
2008-07-10.abstract: Seven international peacekeepers were killed and 22 injured in an ambush in the Sudanese province of Darfur on Tuesday, according to United Nations officials.

2008-07-10.title: Global Warming Talks Leave Few Concrete Goals
2008-07-10.abstract: The sobering reality behind the G-8 summit was that it ended without an agreement on firm targets.

2008-07-10.title: A More Confident Iraq Becomes a Tougher Negotiating Partner for the U.S.
2008-07-10.abstract: Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki’s once enfeebled government, emboldened by several recent military successes, is eager to assert its sovereignty.

2008-07-10.title: U.S. Candidates Use Iran’s Missile Tests as a Chance for a Foreign Policy Debate
2008-07-10.abstract: Barack Obama called for “direct and aggressive diplomacy” with Iran, while John McCain said that the tests highlighted the need for a missile defense system in Europe.

2008-07-10.title: Iraq Military Commander Survives Suicide Bomb Attack
2008-07-10.abstract: Eight Iraqis were killed in a suicide attack against Iraq’s top military commander in the northern city of Mosul. The commander escaped unharmed.

2008-07-11.title: Racing Where Genghis Khan Once Galloped
2008-07-11.abstract: Horse racing has become more popular on the steppes of Mongolia, and a competition is of much greater interest there than the Olympics.

2008-07-11.title: Petraeus Wins Confirmation for New Post
2008-07-11.abstract: The Senate confirmed Gen. David H. Petraeus as commanding officer of the Central Command, giving him vast responsibilities across North Africa, the Middle East and Central Asia.

2008-07-11.title: Four Detained After Deadly Istanbul Attack
2008-07-11.abstract: Authorities refused to elaborate on any possible organizational links behind the shooting, the first attack on an embassy in Turkey in five years.

2008-07-11.title: The Pursuit of Justice vs. the Pursuit of Peace
2008-07-11.abstract: The prosecutor of the International Criminal Court will soon ask judges for an arrest warrant for Sudan's president, Omar Hassan al-Bashir.

2008-07-11.title: Court Cases and Demonstrations Put a New Government to the Test in Thailand
2008-07-11.abstract: A dispute with Cambodia dispute has become a vehicle for growing pressure on Thailand’s government as the divisions that led to a coup in September 2006 have begun to resurface.

2008-07-12.title: Iraqis Return to a Destroyed Village
2008-07-12.abstract: Families that fled a Diyala village have returned, which has offered a glimpse of the difficulties that lie ahead.

2008-07-12.title: Iraqis Return to a Destroyed Village
2008-07-12.abstract: Families that fled a Diyala village have returned, which has offered a glimpse of the difficulties that lie ahead.

2008-07-12.title: 2 Vetoes Quash U.N. Sanctions on Zimbabwe
2008-07-12.abstract: An American-led effort to impose sanctions against Zimbabwe failed in the Security Council, with Russia and China exercising a rare double veto.

2008-07-12.title: Czechs See Oil Flow Fall and Suspect Russian Ire on Missile System
2008-07-12.abstract: After the Czech Republic signed an agreement with the U.S. on an antiballistic missile system that Russia opposes, Prague said the flow of Russian oil was beginning to dwindle.

2008-07-12.title: Pursuit of Sudan’s Leader Incites Debate
2008-07-12.abstract: At the United Nations, tense meetings were held on whether indictment against President Omar Hassan al-Bashir of Sudan should proceed.

2008-07-13.title: A Boy Jockey’s Last Ride in Mongolia
2008-07-13.abstract: Munkherdene, 13, is among a growing number of urban, affluent Mongolians trying to rediscover their nomadic roots by hitting the summer horse-racing circuit.

2008-07-13.title: Colombia Trade Deal Is Threatened
2008-07-13.abstract: The trade agreement remains a long shot, because of opposition by American labor unions, Democratic leaders in Congress and Senator Barack Obama.

2008-07-13.title: In Changing Face of Beijing, a Look at the New China
2008-07-13.abstract: The aura of China’s new architecture has as much to do with intellectual ferment as economic clout.

2008-07-13.title: U.S. Soldiers No Longer Find Haven in Canada
2008-07-13.abstract: Canada’s efforts to remove Americans who deserted from Iraq contrast with the reception for Vietnam deserters.

2008-07-13.title: Korea Negotiators Agree on Nuclear Arms Inspections
2008-07-13.abstract: Negotiators agreed to a blueprint for verifying North Korea’s nuclear disarmament, giving fresh momentum to the talks.

2008-07-14.title: Sarkozy Helps to Bring Syria Out of Isolation
2008-07-14.abstract: French President Nicolas Sarkozy’s “Union for the Mediterranean” represented an end to the diplomatic isolation of President Bashar al-Assad of Syria, who has long been ostracized by the West.

2008-07-14.title: Bronislaw Geremek, Who Helped End Communist Control of Poland, Is Dead at 76
2008-07-14.abstract: Mr. Geremek was a pivotal figure in the fight to end Communist rule in Poland and one of the leading statesmen of the democratic era that followed.

2008-07-14.title: In Japan, Buddhism May Be Dying Out
2008-07-14.abstract: By appearing to cater more to the needs of the dead than to the living, Buddhism may be losing standing in Japan.

2008-07-14.title: Pakistan Marble Helps Taliban Stay in Business
2008-07-14.abstract: The takeover of the Ziarat quarry has enabled the Taliban to turn themselves into a self-sustaining fighting force.

2008-07-14.title: Man Arrested After Attack in Istanbul Is Charged
2008-07-14.abstract: One of the suspects detained in connection with the attack on the United States Consulate in Istanbul was formally charged with membership in an illegal organization.

2008-07-15.title: Czech Republic: Russian Denies Motive
2008-07-15.abstract: Russia denied that politics were behind a sudden reduction in oil deliveries to the Czech Republic late last week.

2008-07-15.title: In France, Syrian Stirs Tensions
2008-07-15.abstract: President Bashar al-Assad of Syria was a guest of honor at France’s annual Bastille Day parade, in a sign that his diplomatic isolation is ending.

2008-07-15.title: South Korea Recalls Envoy to Japan
2008-07-15.abstract: The government of South Korea said it was an “intolerable act” that Japan restated its territorial claim in a new guideline for teachers and textbook publishers.

2008-07-15.title: Israel: Setback for Governing Coalition
2008-07-15.abstract: The governing coalition of Prime Minister Ehud Olmert came under further strain when the Labor Party, a main coalition partner, joined a no-confidence motion in Parliament.

2008-07-15.title: One More Battle for a Vintage Warship
2008-07-15.abstract: An abandoned destroyer finds itself in what could be its final battle, one that could turn the historic ship into a museum or, alternatively, a heap of scrap.

2008-07-16.title: At Royal Balinese Funeral, Bodies Burn and Souls Fly
2008-07-16.abstract: In the most spectacular royal funeral in Bali in at least three decades, three royal figures and 68 commoners were mass cremated.

2008-07-16.title: Prisoner Deal Reopens an Israeli Wound
2008-07-16.abstract: The prospect of exchanging one of the country’s most despised prisoners for corpses has raised hackles.

2008-07-16.title: Bush Emphasizes His Opposition to Timetable for Iraq Withdrawal
2008-07-16.abstract: Remarks by President Bush reflected the growing doubt that the U.S. could negotiate an agreement that would clear the way for American troops to operate in Iraq for many years.

2008-07-16.title: Turkey: Kurdish Rebels Killed
2008-07-16.abstract: Turkish soldiers killed 22 Kurdish separatist rebels in a three-day operation near the border with Iraq, the army said in a statement.

2008-07-16.title: Australia: Catholics’ Youth Gathering Begins
2008-07-16.abstract: World Youth Day, an event that the Roman Catholic Church describes as the largest gathering of young people on the planet, began in a festive atmosphere in Sydney.

2008-07-17.title: Hezbollah Celebrates Release
2008-07-17.abstract: The militant group held a huge celebration after Israel hands over Lebanese prisoners.

2008-07-17.title: Hezbollah Celebrates Release
2008-07-17.abstract: The militant group held a huge celebration after Israel hands over Lebanese prisoners.

2008-07-17.title: Former Samsung Chief Is Convicted
2008-07-17.abstract: A former chairman of the Samsung Group, Lee Kun-hee, was convicted of tax evasion charges but spared prison.

2008-07-17.title: Warrant for Sudanese Is Talk of Security Council
2008-07-17.abstract: Russia and China voiced concerns about the request for an arrest warrant of Sudan’s president, raising the possibility that the U.N. Security Council could intervene.

2008-07-17.title: Justice Is Swift for Novice Korean Jurors
2008-07-17.abstract: This year South Korea took its first steps toward adopting a jury system, and legal experts say that citizen participation has already had a discernible impact on the legal process.

2008-07-18.title: Pakistani Bear Market Has Investors Raging in the Streets
2008-07-18.abstract: With the Karachi Stock Exchange suffering through its worst losing run in at least 18 years, investors are taking to the streets to show their anger at the new government.

2008-07-18.title: Malaysian Opposition Figure’s Arrest Increases Tensions
2008-07-18.abstract: Anwar Ibrahim said that his arrest may have set back one of the strongest challenges in the country’s history to the dominance of the longtime governing party.

2008-07-18.title: Pope Warns on Environment
2008-07-18.abstract: Pope Benedict XVI said that the world was being scarred and its natural resources used up by humanity’s “insatiable consumption.”

2008-07-18.title: A Daughter of India’s Underclass Rises on Votes That Cross Caste Lines
2008-07-18.abstract: The advance of so-called low-caste politicians has reshaped Indian politics, but no one figure has so shaken up the country’s traditional political order as Kumari Mayawati.

2008-07-18.title: Electrical Risks at Iraq Bases Are Worse Than Said
2008-07-18.abstract: Shoddy work by private contractors is causing more deaths and injuries on U.S. military bases in Iraq than previously acknowledged, according to documents.

2008-07-19.title: Poland's Car Culture
2008-07-19.abstract: A look at drag racing in Poland's second-largest city, now organized by the government in response to dangerous illegal races.

2008-07-19.title: China's Newest Fitness Sport
2008-07-19.abstract: A growing number of Chinese women are experimenting with the country's newest, and most controversial, fitness activity.

2008-07-19.title: China's Newest Fitness Sport
2008-07-19.abstract: A growing number of Chinese women are experimenting with the country's newest, and most controversial, fitness activity.

2008-07-19.title: Poland's Car Culture
2008-07-19.abstract: A look at drag racing in Poland's second-largest city, now organized by the government in response to dangerous illegal races.

2008-07-19.title: U.N. May Name South African to Be Rights Chief
2008-07-19.abstract: A South African lawyer, Navanethem Pillay, is being considered to succeed Louise Arbour as High Commissioner for Human Rights, according to a diplomat.

2008-07-20.title: Anatoly I. Pristavkin, 76, Russian Writer, Is Dead
2008-07-20.abstract: Mr. Pristavkin’s experience as a Russian orphan during World War II showed in the suffering of the children in his novels and in the toughness of his opposition to the Soviet authorities.

2008-07-20.title: Sunnis End Boycott and Rejoin Iraqi Government
2008-07-20.abstract: The political move came as Prime Minister Gordon Brown of Britain made an unannounced visit to Iraq.

2008-07-20.title: Somali Killings of Aid Workers Imperil Relief
2008-07-20.abstract: Aid workers whom Somalis depend on are fleeing — driven out by what appears to be an organized terror campaign.

2008-07-20.title: Pope Calls for a ‘New Age’ in Final Australia Mass
2008-07-20.abstract: Pope Benedict XVI attacked the violence and materialism of the modern age in an address to a congregation of about 400,000 gathered in Sydney.

2008-07-20.title: Brown Meets With Iraqi Officials in Baghdad
2008-07-20.abstract: British Prime Minister Gordon Brown made an unannounced visit to Iraq and held talks with Iraqi Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki.

2008-07-21.title: Comment Stings Iraqi Leader on Eve of Obama Visit
2008-07-21.abstract: Iraqi Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki tried to step back from comments in which he appeared to support Senator Barack Obama’s plan for troop withdrawal.

2008-07-21.title: Berlin Ends Fashion Week, Eyes on Glamorous Past
2008-07-21.abstract: Sunday marked just about the only time that Berlin, an aspiring fashion hub and perennial economic also-ran, could safely claim it was the center of the fashion world.

2008-07-21.title: U.S. and NATO Forces Kill 13 Afghans in Strikes Said to Be Mistakes
2008-07-21.abstract: United States and NATO missile strikes continued to exact a heavy toll over the weekend, with at least 13 Afghans killed in two episodes that Afghan officials said were mistakes.

2008-07-21.title: Thai-Cambodian Temple Standoff Continues
2008-07-21.abstract: Soldiers faced off at the ruins of an ancient Hindu temple for a sixth straight day, in a modern-day echo of the age-old clash of empires across Indochina.

2008-07-21.title: Anglican Bishops Meet in Canterbury
2008-07-21.abstract: This year’s Lambeth Conference, which takes place once every 10 years, comes as the church’s ranks have been cut by a battle over gay priests.

2008-07-22.title: Senator Barack Obama Visits Iraq
2008-07-22.abstract: Senator Barack Obama visits American and Iraqi officials in Iraq, discussing Iraqi sovereignty and the withdrawal of American troops.

2008-07-22.title: Bosnian Serb Under Arrest in War Crimes
2008-07-22.abstract: The arrest of Radovan Karadzic, one of the most wanted war criminals for the massacre of nearly 8,000 Muslim men and boys, ended a 13-year hunt.

2008-07-22.title: Putin Orders Restored Oil Flow to Czechs
2008-07-22.abstract: The Czech Republic’s flow of oil from Russia was reduced after the Czechs signed an agreement with the U.S. to base a missile defense radar on their territory.

2008-07-22.title: Unilateral Action by U.S. a Growing Fear in Pakistan
2008-07-22.abstract: Suggestions that the U.S. could resort to unilateral intervention against Al Qaeda and the Taliban in Pakistan have caused anxiety in Pakistan.

2008-07-22.title: China Begins Pulling Soldiers Out of Quake Zone
2008-07-22.abstract: The pullout from China’s earthquake zone marks a shift from short-term recovery efforts to long-term reconstruction work.

2008-07-23.title: Karadzic Is Arrested
2008-07-23.abstract: One of the world's most wanted war criminals, Radovan Karadzic, was arrested in Belgrade.

2008-07-23.title: Karadzic Arrest Is Big Step for a Land Tired of Being Europe’s Pariah
2008-07-23.abstract: The arrest of a wartime Bosnian Serb leader was an unlikely transformation in a country that had appeared to be on a path toward virulent nationalism and isolation.

2008-07-23.title: Dinko Sakic, Who Led WWII Death Camp, Dies at 86
2008-07-23.abstract: Mr. Sakic was found guilty of killing more than 2,000 Serbs, Jews and Gypsies at the concentration camp named Jasenovac.

2008-07-23.title: Talks to Start on Zimbabwe Crisis
2008-07-23.abstract: South Africa is hoping to convene negotiations between Zimbabwe’s feuding political parties on Tuesday, one day after their leaders met face to face.

2008-07-23.title: Rwanda Also Awaits Justice
2008-07-23.abstract: A year before the Srebrenica massacre, a horrible slaughter was unfolding in another part of the world, where the wheels of justice have churned even more slowly.

2008-07-24.title: In First Meeting, Rice Presses North Korean on Nuclear Effort
2008-07-24.abstract: Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice met with her North Korean counterpart for the first time, and prodded Pyongyang to move quickly to dismantle its nuclear arms program.

2008-07-24.title: China Sets Zones for Olympics Protests
2008-07-24.abstract: The Chinese government will permit public protests inside three city parks, but demonstrators must obtain permits and abide by laws that usually make political demonstration nearly impossible.

2008-07-24.title: Obama Meets With Olmert and Abbas
2008-07-24.abstract: Barack Obama said it was in Israel’s interest to find peace with the Palestinians, but he said Israel should be able to defend itself.

2008-07-24.title: Ruling Turns a Village of Winemakers on Itself
2008-07-24.abstract: Families in a village that produces some of Bordeaux’s finest wines have been set against one another by a court ruling challenging the way their wines were classified.

2008-07-24.title: At Trial, Former Serb Leader Seeks to Represent Himself
2008-07-24.abstract: A lawyer for Radovan Karadzic said on Wednesday that his client would defend himself in any war crimes trial if he is handed over to the United Nations tribunal at the Hague.

2008-07-25.title: Obama, Vague on Issues, Pleases Crowd in Europe
2008-07-25.abstract: Many Europeans see Barack Obama’s candidacy as a sign of hope for a more flexible, less ideological America. Governments and politicians are not so sure.

2008-07-25.title: Afghan Soldiers Battle Taliban as NATO Leader Warns of Perils to Nation’s Stability
2008-07-25.abstract: Fierce fighting broke out in southern Afghanistan when scores of Taliban insurgents attacked an Afghan Army convoy on the main highway south of the capital.

2008-07-25.title: State Department Inspector to Investigate Texas Oil Company’s Deal in Kurdistan
2008-07-25.abstract: The department’s internal watchdog will investigate allegations that officials did nothing to stop a deal that undermined both American policy and the Iraqi central government.

2008-07-25.title: 8 Die in Iraq in Suicide Bombing, Apparently by Woman
2008-07-25.abstract: The huge blast on one of Baquba’s main thoroughfares that killed a pro-American Sunni militia leader among others was initially thought to be the result of a car bomb.

2008-07-25.title: Iraqi Athletes ‘Unlikely’ to Be at Olympics
2008-07-25.abstract: Iraq’s refusal to reinstate its official Olympic committee in time to officially submit the names of Iraqi competitors has left most its athletes without a shot of participating in the games.

2008-07-26.title: Back to Art, Havel Meditates on Power
2008-07-26.abstract: Vaclav Havel acknowledges the autobiographical aspects in his new play, which depicts a womanizing former political leader who has grudgingly exited the stage.

2008-07-26.title: Turkey and Greece to Make New Attempt at United Cyprus
2008-07-26.abstract: The Turkish and Greek leaders of Cyprus will convene for peace talks on Sept. 3, for the first time since a referendum to unite the divided island failed in 2004.

2008-07-26.title: Labor Party’s Defeat in Scotland Is Blow to Brown
2008-07-26.abstract: The surprising loss in a by-election raised questions about the future of Gordon Brown, Britain’s prime minister.

2008-07-26.title: U.N. Debates Court Efforts to Prosecute Sudan Chief
2008-07-26.abstract: Some countries voiced concerns that prosecuting Omar Hassan al-Bashir could hamper efforts to achieve peace in the region through diplomacy.

2008-07-26.title: U.S. Strengthens Zimbabwe Sanctions
2008-07-26.abstract: Calling Zimbabwe’s government illegitimate, President Bush ordered the new sanctions to intensify pressure on President Robert Mugabe and his supporters.

2008-07-27.title: A Shiite Militia in Baghdad Sees Its Power Wane
2008-07-27.abstract: The Mahdi Army has been profoundly weakened in a number of neighborhoods, in an important, if tentative, milestone for stability in Iraq.

2008-07-27.title: In Zimbabwe Talks, Who Will Get the Real Power?
2008-07-27.abstract: Negotiators are confronting a seemingly unbridgeable divide over executive power in a unity government.

2008-07-27.title: Cocaine Sustains War in Rural Colombia
2008-07-27.abstract: Colombia’s four-decade war fueled by the drug trade is proving immune to American-financed efforts to stop it.

2008-07-27.title: Blasts in Gaza Stoke Tensions Between Factions
2008-07-27.abstract: Hamas police officers in Gaza rounded up scores of supporters of Fatah and raided its offices after five Hamas militants and a girl were killed in a bomb blast.

2008-07-27.title: Hole in Qantas Jet Is Investigated
2008-07-27.abstract: Investigators sought Saturday to determine why a section of the fuselage of a Boeing jumbo jet burst open in fight, forcing an emergency landing.

2008-07-28.title: U.S. Military Says Soldiers Fired on Civilians
2008-07-28.abstract: The American military admitted that a platoon of soldiers raked a car of Iraqi civilians with gunfire and that the military then issued a misleading news release.

2008-07-28.title: Revered by the Castros and Their Opponents
2008-07-28.abstract: The shrine for the Virgin of El Cobre in Cuba is an essential part of the country’s religious, social and political landscape.

2008-07-28.title: In Firm Control, Cambodia’s Premier Is Poised for Election Victory
2008-07-28.abstract: Prime Minister Hun Sen appeared headed for an expected election victory on Sunday after what experts said was the least violent political campaign in Cambodia’s recent history.

2008-07-28.title: Officials Ask Qantas to Inspect Oxygen Canisters
2008-07-28.abstract: Authorities asked for inspections of oxygen bottles after the fuselage of a Qantas Boeing 747 burst open in flight.

2008-07-28.title: Facing a Wave of Violence, India Is Rattled
2008-07-28.abstract: Recent targets of terrorism had nothing in common — temples, streets, hospitals — making the impersonal nature of the violence new and deeply unsettling.

2008-07-29.title: Bombers and Ethnic Clashes Kill 61 in Iraq
2008-07-29.abstract: Suicide bombers struck Kurdish protesters in Kirkuk and Shiite pilgrims in Baghdad in one of the deadliest series of attacks in Iraq this year.

2008-07-29.title: Arrests Increase Tensions Between Palestinian Factions
2008-07-29.abstract: Tensions grew amid reports that the Fatah-dominated Palestinian Authority security forces were detaining more than 50 activists and academics associated with Hamas.

2008-07-29.title: In Beijing, Blue Skies Prove Hard to Achieve
2008-07-29.abstract: Beijing’s skies are so polluted that authorities are considering emergency measures during the Olympics.

2008-07-29.title: After Bombing, Turkish Leader Urges Unity
2008-07-29.abstract: Prime Minister Recep Tayyip Erdogan, whose party is under judicial pressure, said the country needed to address its more pressing security issues, a day after 17 died in a double bombing.

2008-07-29.title: Ryanair Shares Tumble on Earnings Report
2008-07-29.abstract: Europe’s biggest no-frills carrier warned on Monday that it might post its first annual loss as a public company.

2008-07-30.title: July 2008: Bashir Rallies Supporters
2008-07-30.abstract: President Omar Hassan al-Bashir of Sudan, accused of orchestrating genocide in Darfur, is finding support among his former enemies.

2008-07-30.title: Stronger Than Ever
2008-07-30.abstract: President Omar Hassan al-Bashir of Sudan, accused of orchestrating the genocide in Darfur, is finding support among his former enemies.

2008-07-30.title: Beijing Hides Dilapidated Buildings
2008-07-30.abstract: Beijing has set up walls around unsightly homes and shops in an attempt to “rectify the Olympic environment.”

2008-07-30.title: Corrections: For the Record
2008-07-30.abstract: An article on July 21 about issues confronting the worldwide Anglican Communion during its gathering in Canterbury, England, misstated the background of Katharine Jefferts Schori, the presiding bishop of the Episcopal Church in the United States, who is attending the gathering. She was born in Florida, grew up in the Seattle area and had been the bishop of the Diocese of Nevada before she was elected to her current position in 2006. She is not “of Boston.”.

2008-07-30.title: Australia Announces Changes on Asylum Seekers
2008-07-30.abstract: Australia is ending its controversial policy of automatic detention for asylum seekers who arrive in the country without visas, the government announced Tuesday.

2008-07-31.title: Riding a Wave of Growth
2008-07-31.abstract: Brazil’s economic growth is being felt in nearly all sectors as many citizens move into the middle class.

2008-07-31.title: Turkish Court Calls Ruling Party Constitutional
2008-07-31.abstract: Turkey’s governing party narrowly missed being banned in a court ruling that relieved months of pressure in the country.

2008-07-31.title: Strong Economy Propels Brazil to World Stage
2008-07-31.abstract: Brazil, South America’s largest economy, is finally poised to realize its potential as a global player, economists say.

2008-07-31.title: Penalty for China Quake Photos Reported
2008-07-31.abstract: A school employee who posted his photographs of quake-damaged schools online has been ordered to a labor camp for a year.

2008-07-31.title: Olmert to Quit After Elections in September
2008-07-31.abstract: Embroiled in a corruption inquiry, Israel’s prime minister says he will resign as soon as his party chooses a successor.
